creator: dr53003@alltel.net
text:
im trying to install freebsd on an amd 1000MHz computer with a 40g drive.  i have 20g for bsd.  when i install everything on the cdrom (only the first cdrom in the pack of 10) i get a 'no free inode' error.  I find it hard to believe that i have run out of room on a 20g drive.  also with win 2k on the back 20g's, i cant seem to boot into windows with your boot manager.  thanks.

How-To-Repeat: i can repeat this 100% of the time by simply choosing to install most all of the packages on the cd, and choosing all the files in those packages.  no free inodes and then i cant boot into the system after this happens.  thanks
